35:   | { type: 'tool'; tool: 'write_file'; args: { path: string; content: string } }
36:   | { type: 'tool'; tool: 'run_command'; args: { command: string; timeoutMs?: number } }
37:   | { type: 'tool'; tool: 'diff_summary'; args: { maxBytes?: number } }
38:   | { type: 'final'; summary: string };

67:   }

70: class PolicyError extends Error {
71:   constructor(
72:     public readonly code: string,
73:     message: string
74:   ) {
75:     super(message);
76:     this.name = 'PolicyError';
77:   }

80: function shellQuote(value: string): string {
81:   return `'${value.replace(/'/g, `"'"'`)}'`;
82: }
83: 
84: function assertWorkspacePath(pathInput: string, policy: TaskToolPolicy): string {
85:   const trimmed = (pathInput || '.').trim();
86:   const normalized = trimmed.replace(/\\/g, '/');
87:   if (normalized.includes('\u0000')) {
88:     throw new PolicyError('invalid_path', 'Path contains null bytes');
89:   }
90:   if (normalized.startsWith('/') || normalized.startsWith('..') || normalized.includes('/../')) {
91:     throw new PolicyError('invalid_path', 'Path escapes workspace root');
92:   }
93: 
94:   const collapsed = normalized
95:     .split('/')
96:     .filter((segment) => segment.length > 0 && segment !== '.')
97:     .join('/');
98:   if (collapsed.startsWith('..')) {
99:     throw new PolicyError('invalid_path', 'Path escapes workspace root');
100:   }
101:   if (collapsed === '.git' || collapsed.startsWith('.git/')) {
102:     throw new PolicyError('invalid_path', 'Access to .git is denied by policy');
103:   }
104: 
105:   for (const deny of policy.denyPaths) {
106:     const normalizedDeny = deny.trim().replace(/^\//, '');
107:     if (!normalizedDeny) {
108:       continue;
109:     }
110:     if (normalizedDeny.endsWith('/**')) {
111:       const prefix = normalizedDeny.slice(0, -3);
112:       if (collapsed === prefix || collapsed.startsWith(`${prefix}/`)) {
113:         throw new PolicyError('invalid_path', `Access to '${prefix}' is denied by policy`);
114:       }
115:       continue;
116:     }
117: 
118:     if (collapsed === normalizedDeny || collapsed.startsWith(`${normalizedDeny}/`)) {
119:       throw new PolicyError('invalid_path', `Access to '${normalizedDeny}' is denied by policy`);
120:     }
121:   }
122: 
123:   return `${policy.rootPath}/${collapsed}`;
124: }

126: function sanitizeErrorMessage(input: string): string {
127:   return input
128:     .replace(/(authorization:\s*bearer\s+)[a-z0-9._-]+/gi, '$1[REDACTED]')
129:     .replace(/ghs_[a-z0-9_]+/gi, '[REDACTED_TOKEN]');
130: }

132: function isTransientFailure(error: unknown): boolean {
133:   const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
134:   return /(timeout|timed out|temporar|network|fetch failed|connection reset|sandbox unavailable)/i.test(message);
135: }

137: function parseNumber(value: unknown, fallback: number, min: number, max: number): number {
138:   const source = typeof value === 'number' && Number.isFinite(value) ? value : fallback;
139:   const n = Math.floor(source);
140:   if (n < min) {
141:     return min;
142:   }
143:   if (n > max) {
144:     return max;
145:   }
146:   return n;
147: }
148: 
149: function parseIntegerString(value: string | undefined, fallback: number, min: number, max: number): number {
150:   if (value === undefined) {
151:     return fallback;
152:   }
153:   const parsed = Number.parseInt(value.trim(), 10);
154:   if (Number.isNaN(parsed)) {
155:     return fallback;
156:   }
157:   if (parsed < min) {
158:     return min;
159:   }
160:   if (parsed > max) {
161:     return max;
162:   }
163:   return parsed;
164: }
165: 
166: function parseBooleanString(value: string | undefined, fallback: boolean): boolean {
167:   if (value === undefined) {
168:     return fallback;
169:   }
170:   const normalized = value.trim().toLowerCase();
171:   if (['1', 'true', 'yes', 'on'].includes(normalized)) {
172:     return true;
173:   }
174:   if (['0', 'false', 'no', 'off'].includes(normalized)) {
175:     return false;
176:   }
177:   return fallback;
178: }

180: function normalizeToolPolicy(input: Record<string, unknown> | null): TaskToolPolicy {
181:   const commands = (input?.commands ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
182:   const allowRaw = Array.isArray(commands.allow) ? commands.allow : [];
183:   const denyRaw = Array.isArray(commands.deny) ? commands.deny : [];
184:   const limits = (input?.limits ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
185:   const filePaths = (input?.filePaths ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
186: 
187:   const commandAllow = allowRaw
188:     .filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string')
189:     .map((value) => value.trim())
190:     .filter((value) => value.length > 0);
191: 
192:   const commandDeny = denyRaw
193:     .filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string')
194:     .map((value) => value.trim())
195:     .filter((value) => value.length > 0);
196: 
197:   const maxCommandTimeoutMs = parseNumber(limits.maxCommandTimeoutMs, MAX_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MS, 1_000, MAX_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MS);
198:   const maxOutputBytes = parseNumber(limits.maxOutputBytes, 32_000, 1_024, 512_000);
199:   const rootPathRaw = typeof filePaths.root === 'string' ? filePaths.root.trim() : WORKSPACE_ROOT;
200:   const rootPath = rootPathRaw.startsWith('/') ? rootPathRaw : WORKSPACE_ROOT;
201:   const denyPaths = Array.isArray(filePaths.deny)
202:     ? filePaths.deny.filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string')
203:     : ['.git/**'];
204: 
205:   return {
206:     commandAllow,
207:     commandDeny,
208:     maxCommandTimeoutMs,
209:     maxOutputBytes,
210:     rootPath,
211:     denyPaths,
212:   };
213: }

215: class ScriptedAgentProvider implements AgentProvider {
216:   private readonly actions: AgentAction[];
217: 
218:   constructor(actions: AgentAction[]) {
219:     this.actions = actions;
220:   }
221: 
222:   async next(input: { step: number }): Promise<AgentAction> {
223:     if (input.step - 1 >= this.actions.length) {
224:       return { type: 'final', summary: 'Scripted task completed' };
225:     }
226: 
227:     return this.actions[input.step - 1];
228:   }
229: }

231: class CloudflareAgentSdkProvider implements AgentProvider {
232:   constructor(
233:     private readonly endpoint: string,
234:     private readonly authToken: string | null
235:   ) {}
236: 
237:   async next(input: {
238:     prompt: string;
239:     model: string;
240:     maxSteps: number;
241:     step: number;
242:     history: AgentHistoryEntry[];
243:   }): Promise<AgentAction> {
244:     const response = await fetch(this.endpoint, {
245:       method: 'POST',
246:       headers: {
247:         'Content-Type': 'application/json',
248:         ...(this.authToken ? { Authorization: `Bearer ${this.authToken}` } : {}),
249:       },
250:       body: JSON.stringify({
251:         mode: 'workspace_task',
252:         prompt: input.prompt,
253:         model: input.model,
254:         maxSteps: input.maxSteps,
255:         step: input.step,
256:         history: input.history,
257:       }),
258:     });
259: 
260:     if (!response.ok) {
261:       if (response.status >= 500 || response.status === 429) {
262:         throw new QueueRetryError(`Cloudflare Agent SDK request failed with status ${response.status}`);
263:       }
264:       throw new Error(`Cloudflare Agent SDK request failed with status ${response.status}`);
265:     }
266: 
267:     const parsed = (await response.json()) as unknown;
268:     if (!parsed || typeof parsed !== 'object' || Array.isArray(parsed)) {
269:       throw new Error('Cloudflare Agent SDK returned invalid payload');
270:     }
271: 
272:     const action = (parsed as { action?: unknown }).action;
273:     if (!action || typeof action !== 'object' || Array.isArray(action)) {
274:       throw new Error('Cloudflare Agent SDK response missing action');
275:     }
276: 
277:     return validateAgentAction(action);
278:   }
279: }
280: 
281: function validateAgentAction(action: unknown): AgentAction {
282:   if (!action || typeof action !== 'object' || Array.isArray(action)) {
283:     throw new PolicyError('invalid_action', 'Agent action must be an object');
284:   }
285: 
286:   const record = action as Record<string, unknown>;
287:   if (record.type === 'final') {
288:     const summary = typeof record.summary === 'string' ? record.summary.trim() : '';
289:     if (!summary) {
290:       throw new PolicyError('invalid_action', 'Final action requires a non-empty summary');
291:     }
292:     return { type: 'final', summary };
293:   }
294: 
295:   if (record.type !== 'tool') {
296:     throw new PolicyError('invalid_action', 'Action type must be tool or final');
297:   }
298: 
299:   const tool = typeof record.tool === 'string' ? record.tool : '';
300:   const args =
301:     record.args && typeof record.args === 'object' && !Array.isArray(record.args)
302:       ? (record.args as Record<string, unknown>)
303:       : {};
304: 
305:   switch (tool) {
306:     case 'list_files':
307:       if (args.path !== undefined && typeof args.path !== 'string') {
308:         throw new PolicyError('invalid_action', 'list_files.path must be a string when provided');
309:       }
310:       return {
311:         type: 'tool',
312:         tool,
313:         args,
314:       } as AgentAction;
315:     case 'read_file':
316:       if (typeof args.path !== 'string' || !args.path.trim()) {
317:         throw new PolicyError('invalid_action', 'read_file.path is required');
318:       }
319:       if (args.maxBytes !== undefined && (typeof args.maxBytes !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(args.maxBytes))) {
320:         throw new PolicyError('invalid_action', 'read_file.maxBytes must be a number when provided');
321:       }
322:       return {
323:         type: 'tool',
324:         tool,
325:         args,
326:       } as AgentAction;
327:     case 'write_file':
328:       if (typeof args.path !== 'string' || !args.path.trim()) {
329:         throw new PolicyError('invalid_action', 'write_file.path is required');
330:       }
331:       if (typeof args.content !== 'string') {
332:         throw new PolicyError('invalid_action', 'write_file.content must be a string');
333:       }
334:       return {
335:         type: 'tool',
336:         tool,
337:         args,
338:       } as AgentAction;
339:     case 'run_command':
340:       if (typeof args.command !== 'string' || !args.command.trim()) {
341:         throw new PolicyError('invalid_action', 'run_command.command is required');
342:       }
343:       if (args.timeoutMs !== undefined && (typeof args.timeoutMs !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(args.timeoutMs))) {
344:         throw new PolicyError('invalid_action', 'run_command.timeoutMs must be a number when provided');
345:       }
346:       return {
347:         type: 'tool',
348:         tool,
349:         args,
350:       } as AgentAction;
351:     case 'diff_summary':
352:       if (args.maxBytes !== undefined && (typeof args.maxBytes !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(args.maxBytes))) {
353:         throw new PolicyError('invalid_action', 'diff_summary.maxBytes must be a number when provided');
354:       }
355:       return {
356:         type: 'tool',
357:         tool,
358:         args,
359:       } as AgentAction;
360:     default:
361:       throw new PolicyError('invalid_tool', `Tool '${tool}' is not supported`);
362:   }
363: }

378: function enforceCommandPolicy(command: string, policy: TaskToolPolicy): void {
379:   const trimmed = command.trim();
380:   if (!trimmed) {
381:     throw new PolicyError('command_not_allowed', 'Command cannot be empty');
382:   }
383: 
384:   const normalizedCommand = trimmed.toLowerCase();
385:   if (policy.commandDeny.some((entry) => normalizedCommand.includes(entry.toLowerCase()))) {
386:     throw new PolicyError('command_denied', 'Command denied by policy');
387:   }
388: 
389:   if (/[;&|`<>\n\r]/.test(trimmed) || /\$\(|\)\s*\{/.test(trimmed)) {
390:     throw new PolicyError('command_denied', 'Command contains denied shell metacharacters');
391:   }
392: 
393:   const allowEntries = policy.commandAllow;
394:   const allowed = allowEntries.some((entry) => {
395:     const normalizedEntry = entry.toLowerCase();
396:     if (normalizedEntry.includes(' ')) {
397:       return normalizedCommand === normalizedEntry || normalizedCommand.startsWith(`${normalizedEntry} `);
398:     }
399:     return normalizedCommand === normalizedEntry || normalizedCommand.startsWith(`${normalizedEntry} `);
400:   });
401:   if (!allowed) {
402:     throw new PolicyError('command_not_allowed', 'Command is not in allowlist');
403:   }
404: }
405: 
406: async function runSandboxCommand(
407:   sandbox: SandboxClient,
408:   command: string,
409:   timeout?: number
410: ): Promise<{ stdout: string; stderr: string }> {
411:   const result = await sandbox.exec(command, { timeout });
412:   if (result.exitCode !== 0) {
413:     const output = [result.stdout, result.stderr].filter(Boolean).join('\n');
414:     throw new Error(`Sandbox command failed with exit ${result.exitCode}: ${output || 'No output'}`);
415:   }
416:   return { stdout: result.stdout, stderr: result.stderr };
417: }
418: 
419: async function executeTool(
420:   sandbox: SandboxClient,
421:   action: Extract<AgentAction, { type: 'tool' }>,
422:   maxCommandTimeoutMs: number,
423:   policy: TaskToolPolicy
424: ): Promise<unknown> {
425:   if (action.tool === 'list_files') {
426:     const absolutePath = assertWorkspacePath(action.args.path ?? '.', policy);
427:     const output = await runSandboxCommand(
428:       sandbox,
429:       `python3 - ${shellQuote(absolutePath)} <<'PY'\nimport json\nimport os\nimport sys\n\npath = sys.argv[1]\nif not os.path.exists(path):\n    print(json.dumps({'error':'not_found'}))\n    raise SystemExit(0)\nentries = []\nfor name in sorted(os.listdir(path)):\n    full = os.path.join(path, name)\n    entries.append({'name': name, 'type': 'directory' if os.path.isdir(full) else 'file'})\nprint(json.dumps({'entries': entries[:500]}))\nPY`
430:     );
431:     return JSON.parse(output.stdout || '{}');
432:   }
433: 
434:   if (action.tool === 'read_file') {
435:     const absolutePath = assertWorkspacePath(action.args.path, policy);
436:     const maxBytes = parseNumber(action.args.maxBytes, 200_000, 1, 2_000_000);
437:     const output = await runSandboxCommand(
438:       sandbox,
439:       `python3 - ${shellQuote(absolutePath)} ${maxBytes} <<'PY'\nimport json\nimport os\nimport sys\n\npath = sys.argv[1]\nmax_bytes = int(sys.argv[2])\nif not os.path.exists(path):\n    print(json.dumps({'error':'not_found'}))\n    raise SystemExit(0)\nwith open(path, 'rb') as f:\n    data = f.read(max_bytes + 1)\ntruncated = len(data) > max_bytes\nif truncated:\n    data = data[:max_bytes]\ntext = data.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')\nprint(json.dumps({'content': text, 'truncated': truncated, 'bytes': len(data)}))\nPY`
440:     );
441:     return JSON.parse(output.stdout || '{}');
442:   }
443: 
444:   if (action.tool === 'write_file') {
445:     const absolutePath = assertWorkspacePath(action.args.path, policy);
446:     const directory = absolutePath.slice(0, absolutePath.lastIndexOf('/')) || WORKSPACE_ROOT;
447:     await runSandboxCommand(sandbox, `mkdir -p ${shellQuote(directory)}`);
448:     await sandbox.writeFile(absolutePath, action.args.content);
449:     return { ok: true, path: action.args.path, bytes: new TextEncoder().encode(action.args.content).length };
450:   }
451: 
452:   if (action.tool === 'run_command') {
453:     enforceCommandPolicy(action.args.command, policy);
454:     const timeout = parseNumber(action.args.timeoutMs, DEFAULT_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_MS, 1_000, maxCommandTimeoutMs);
455:     const output = await runSandboxCommand(
456:       sandbox,
457:       `cd ${shellQuote(WORKSPACE_ROOT)} && ${action.args.command}`,
458:       timeout
459:     );
460:     return {
461:       stdout: output.stdout.slice(0, policy.maxOutputBytes),
462:       stderr: output.stderr.slice(0, policy.maxOutputBytes),
463:       truncated: output.stdout.length > policy.maxOutputBytes || output.stderr.length > policy.maxOutputBytes,
464:     };
465:   }
466: 
467:   if (action.tool === 'diff_summary') {
468:     const maxBytes = parseNumber(action.args.maxBytes, 128_000, 1_024, policy.maxOutputBytes);
469:     const output = await runSandboxCommand(
470:       sandbox,
471:       `cd ${shellQuote(WORKSPACE_ROOT)} && git diff --name-status && echo '__NIMBUS_PATCH__' && git diff`
472:     );
473:     const marker = '__NIMBUS_PATCH__';
474:     const markerIndex = output.stdout.indexOf(marker);
475:     const header = markerIndex >= 0 ? output.stdout.slice(0, markerIndex).trim() : '';
476:     const patch = markerIndex >= 0 ? output.stdout.slice(markerIndex + marker.length).trim() : output.stdout.trim();
477:     return {
478:       changedFiles: header ? header.split(/\r?\n/).filter((line) => line.trim().length > 0) : [],
479:       patch: patch.length > maxBytes ? patch.slice(0, maxBytes) : patch,
480:       truncated: patch.length > maxBytes,
481:     };
482:   }
483: 
484:   throw new PolicyError('invalid_tool', 'Unsupported tool');
485: }

487: function createProvider(env: Env, payload: Record<string, unknown>, providerName: string): AgentProvider {
488:   if (providerName === 'scripted') {
489:     const allowScriptedProvider = parseBooleanString(env.WORKSPACE_AGENT_ALLOW_SCRIPTED_PROVIDER, false);
490:     if (!allowScriptedProvider) {
491:       throw new PolicyError('scripted_provider_disabled', 'Scripted provider is disabled');
492:     }
493:     const scriptedActionsRaw = payload.scriptedActions;
494:     if (!Array.isArray(scriptedActionsRaw)) {
495:       return new ScriptedAgentProvider([]);
496:     }
497:     return new ScriptedAgentProvider(scriptedActionsRaw as AgentAction[]);
498:   }
499: 
500:   if (providerName === 'cloudflare_agents_sdk') {
501:     const endpoint = (env.AGENT_SDK_URL ?? '').trim();
502:     if (!endpoint) {
503:       throw new Error('AGENT_SDK_URL is required for cloudflare_agents_sdk provider');
504:     }
505:     const authToken = (env.AGENT_SDK_AUTH_TOKEN ?? '').trim() || null;
506:     return new CloudflareAgentSdkProvider(endpoint, authToken);
507:   }
508: 
509:   throw new Error(`Unsupported agent provider: ${providerName}`);
510: }

514:   if (!task) {

520: async function executeWorkspaceTask(
521:   env: Env,
522:   workspaceId: string,
523:   task: WorkspaceTaskResponse,
524:   payload: Record<string, unknown>,
525:   toolPolicy: TaskToolPolicy
526: ): Promise<void> {
527:   const workspace = await getWorkspace(env.DB, workspaceId);
528:   if (!workspace || workspace.status !== 'ready') {
529:     throw new Error('Workspace is not ready for task execution');
530:   }
531: 
532:   const provider = createProvider(env, payload, task.provider);
533:   const sandbox = await sandboxResolver(env, workspace.sandboxId);
534:   const history: AgentHistoryEntry[] = [];
535:   const maxCommandTimeoutMs = parseIntegerString(
536:     env.WORKSPACE_AGENT_TIMEOUT_MS,
537:     toolPolicy.maxCommandTimeoutMs,
538:     1_000,
539:     toolPolicy.maxCommandTimeoutMs
540:   );
541: 
542:   await appendWorkspaceTaskEvent(env.DB, {
543:     workspaceId,
544:     taskId: task.id,
545:     eventType: 'task_started',
546:     payload: {
547:       provider: task.provider,
548:       model: task.model,
549:       attemptCount: task.attemptCount,
550:       maxSteps: task.maxSteps,
551:     },
552:   });
553: 
554:   for (let step = 1; step <= task.maxSteps; step += 1) {
555:     if (await isCancelRequested(env, workspaceId, task.id)) {
556:       await updateWorkspaceTaskStatus(env.DB, task.id, 'cancelled', {
557:         workspaceId,
558:         result: { reason: 'cancel_requested', step },
559:         errorCode: null,
560:         errorMessage: null,
561:       });
562:       try {
563:         await appendWorkspaceTaskEvent(env.DB, {
564:           workspaceId,
565:           taskId: task.id,
566:           eventType: 'task_cancelled',
567:           payload: { step },
568:         });
569:       } catch {
570:         // Best-effort event append after terminal status.
571:       }
572:       return;
573:     }
574: 
575:     const action = await provider.next({
576:       prompt: task.prompt,
577:       model: task.model,
578:       maxSteps: task.maxSteps,
579:       step,
580:       history,
581:     });
582: 
583:     if (action.type === 'final') {
584:       await updateWorkspaceTaskStatus(env.DB, task.id, 'succeeded', {
585:         workspaceId,
586:         result: { summary: action.summary, stepsExecuted: step - 1 },
587:         errorCode: null,
588:         errorMessage: null,
589:       });
590:       try {
591:         await appendWorkspaceTaskEvent(env.DB, {
592:           workspaceId,
593:           taskId: task.id,
594:           eventType: 'task_succeeded',
595:           payload: { summary: action.summary, stepsExecuted: step - 1 },
596:         });
597:       } catch {
598:         // Best-effort event append after terminal status.
599:       }
600:       return;
601:     }
602: 
603:     await appendWorkspaceTaskEvent(env.DB, {
604:       workspaceId,
605:       taskId: task.id,
606:       eventType: 'tool_call_started',
607:       payload: {
608:         step,
609:         tool: action.tool,
610:       },
611:     });
612: 
613:     const toolOutput = await executeTool(sandbox, action, maxCommandTimeoutMs, toolPolicy);
614:     history.push({ role: 'assistant', content: `tool:${action.tool}` });
615:     history.push({ role: 'tool', tool: action.tool, output: toolOutput });
616: 
617:     await appendWorkspaceTaskEvent(env.DB, {
618:       workspaceId,
619:       taskId: task.id,
620:       eventType: 'tool_call_completed',
621:       payload: {
622:         step,
623:         tool: action.tool,
624:         output: toolOutput,
625:       },
626:     });

632: export async function processWorkspaceTask(env: Env, workspaceId: string, taskId: string): Promise<void> {

642:   try {
643:     const flags = await loadRuntimeFlags(env);

650:       await appendWorkspaceTaskEvent(env.DB, {

652:         taskId,

657:         },
658:       });
659:       return;
660:     }
661: 
662:     task = await getWorkspaceTask(env.DB, workspaceId, taskId);
663:     if (!task) {

665:     }
666: 
667:     const payload = await getWorkspaceTaskRequestPayload(env.DB, taskId);
668:     const toolPolicyInput = await getWorkspaceTaskToolPolicy(env.DB, workspaceId, taskId);

671:         workspaceId,
672:         errorCode: 'task_not_found',
673:         errorMessage: 'Workspace task payload no longer exists',
674:       });
675:       return;
676:     }
677: 
678:     const toolPolicy = normalizeToolPolicy(toolPolicyInput);
679:     await executeWorkspaceTask(env, workspaceId, task, payload, toolPolicy);
680:   } catch (error) {
681:     const freshTask = await getWorkspaceTask(env.DB, workspaceId, taskId);
682:     const attemptCount = freshTask?.attemptCount ?? task?.attemptCount ?? 0;
683:     const maxRetries = freshTask?.maxRetries ?? task?.maxRetries ?? 0;
684: 
685:     if (freshTask?.cancelRequestedAt) {
686:       await updateWorkspaceTaskStatus(env.DB, taskId, 'cancelled', {

694:         taskId,

698:       return;
699:     }

702:       await updateWorkspaceTaskStatus(env.DB, taskId, 'failed', {
703:         workspaceId,
704:         errorCode: error.code,
705:         errorMessage: error.message,
706:       });
707:       await appendWorkspaceTaskEvent(env.DB, {
708:         workspaceId,
709:         taskId,

712:       });
713:       return;

718:         workspaceId,

731:         },
732:       });

734:     }
735: 
736:     const message = sanitizeErrorMessage(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
737:     await updateWorkspaceTaskStatus(env.DB, taskId, 'failed', {
738:       workspaceId,
739:       errorCode: 'task_execution_failed',
740:       errorMessage: message,
741:     });
742:     await appendWorkspaceTaskEvent(env.DB, {
743:       workspaceId,
744:       taskId,
745:       eventType: 'task_failed',
746:       payload: {
747:         code: 'task_execution_failed',
748:         message,
749:       },
750:     });
751:   }
752: }

755:   if (error instanceof QueueRetryError) {

759:   const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);

762:   }

state.title: packages/worker/src/lib/db.ts

parts.text:
Found two correctness issues worth fixing before merge:

- **High – unsupported deploy providers are silently accepted but executed as simulated**
  - In `handleCreateWorkspaceDeployment`, `provider` is taken verbatim from request and persisted (`packages/worker/src/api/workspace-deployments.ts:143`), but the runner never branches on provider and always performs the simulated artifact flow (`packages/worker/src/lib/workspace-deployment-runner.ts:284` onward).
  - Result: a request like `"provider": "vercel"` returns/records a successful deployment that was not actually executed by that provider, which is misleading and breaks provenance expectations.
  - Fix: validate allowed providers at create time (currently likely only `simulated`) and return `400` for unknown values, or implement provider dispatch in the runner.

- **Medium – `finishedAt` option is effectively ignored for terminal status updates**
  - `updateWorkspaceDeploymentStatus` can append both `finished_at = ?` (when `options.finishedAt` is provided) and later `finished_at = COALESCE(finished_at, ?)` for terminal states (`packages/worker/src/lib/db.ts:2010` and `packages/worker/src/lib/db.ts:2044`).
  - In SQLite, the later assignment wins, so the explicit `finishedAt` passed by callers is not reliably applied.
  - Fix: compute one final `finished_at` assignment path (e.g., use provided `finishedAt` if present, otherwise `COALESCE(finished_at, now)`), then compute `duration_ms` from the same timestamp.

I couldn’t run the worker test suite in this environment because `npm` is broken locally (`Cannot find module 'node:path'`), so this review is based on static inspection.
